message:
test(fcp-raptorq): pin ESI no-collision invariant + use checked_add at sites (br-gh7gt)

Close flywheel_connectors-gh7gt. The prior fix (c3cc8695) added a cap
`repair_count = repair_symbols.min(u32::MAX - k_prime)` which
mathematically prevents the `k_prime + i` overflow flagged in the bead.
That guard is correct, but the raw `u32 + u32` add remained at the two
repair-ESI emission sites in encode.rs — so a future regression that
weakened or removed the cap would silently wrap into the source-symbol
ESI range [0, k_prime) in release builds, producing duplicate ESIs that
collide with the systematic source symbols. Debug builds would panic on
overflow with an unhelpful message.

## Fix

crates/fcp-raptorq/src/encode.rs

Replace `let esi = k_prime + i` at `encode_all` (line 149) and
`into_encode_all` (line 174) with:

  let esi = k_prime
      .checked_add(i)
      .expect("repair_count cap ensures k_prime + i stays within u32");

This expresses the invariant at the use site (matching the
saturating/checked discipline used in config.rs:155, decode.rs:585,
decode.rs:598) and converts a future cap regression from a silent
wrap-and-collide in release into a loud panic during construction —
which is the conservative failure mode for a supply-path-adjacent
codec.

## Regression test

New `encode_all_repair_esis_never_collide_with_source_range` pins the
two halves of the contract on a realistic 6,400-byte payload (100
source symbols + 5 repair symbols at the 5% ratio):

  1. `encode_all` emits no duplicate ESIs — the HashSet check would
     trip on any wrap collision.
  2. Every repair ESI sits at or above `k_prime` — a wrap regression
     would push at least one repair ESI below `k_prime` and into the
     source range.

Both assertions are repeated for `into_encode_all` so the consuming
variant is covered by the same gate.

The bead notes "LOW" severity because the cap currently makes the bug
unreachable; this commit is defensive-depth that preserves uniform
arithmetic discipline across the crate and keeps the invariant pinned
by a test rather than a comment.
